concerns.analysis: Checked for missing, broken, or fabricated links. There are numerous instances where text that is hyperlinked in the source PDF appears as plain text within the structural elements of the JSON (such as table cells and list items). For instance, in the '5 Species Listings' table on page 38, the link 'Implementation Progress' is clickable in the PDF, but in the JSON, it is rendered as plain text inside the table cell. The corresponding URL is merely appended as a floating link element at the very
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table cells containing clickable hyperlinks, such as "James Spinymussel" and "Implementation Progress"

concerns.analysis: Several critical and meaningful maps present in the source PDF are completely missing from the HTML output. Specifically, Figures 3, 4, 5, and 6 (Pages 18-21), which are full-page contaminant concentration and land-use control area maps, are absent. The HTML only contains the extracted text from the legends and notes, along with placeholder text like `[Image: North arrow and graphic scale bar (0 to 40 feet)]`, but no actual `` element displaying the visual map data. Similarly, the survey pl
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 18 contains 'Figure 3: Soil Contaminant Concentration Map', a full-page detailed map showing soil borings, building outlines, and contaminant contours.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output contains text extracted from the page such as 'COMPLETE CLEANERS FACILITY' and '[Image: North arrow and graphic scale bar (0 to 40 feet)]', but there is no element corresponding to the map itself.

concerns.analysis: The bird species table spanning pages 44 and 45 was not extracted as a unified table. The column headers ('NAME', 'BREEDING SEASON...') were extracted as a single-row table at the bottom of page 44, but the data rows on page 45 were completely flattened into sequential headings and paragraphs. This destroys the two-column tabular structure connecting the bird species with their corresponding breeding seasons.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Two-column tabular layout:
NAME: Bald Eagle Haliaeetus leucocephalus
BREEDING SEASON: Breeds Sep 1 to Jul 31

concerns.analysis: The numbered list on page 28 (questions 1 through 4) is a continuous list in the source PDF. However, the extraction pipeline split this into four separate single-item ordered lists, interspersed with paragraph elements for the answers. Because they are extracted as separate list objects, the HTML renderer will restart the numbering at '1.' for every single question instead of continuing sequentially from 1 to 4.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1. Are there navigable water bodies...
Yes, two unnamed tributaries...
2. Are there any water bodies anywhere...

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 56 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=56, examples=['https://harthick.sharepoint.com/sites/MasterFiles-1/Shared D', 'http://nwi.fws.gov/', 'https://fws.maps.arcgis.com/home/webmap/print.html']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (109): i; ii; https://harthick.sharepoint.com/sites/MasterFiles-1/Shared D
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=109, pages_affected=51/73. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 3 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://www.fws.gov/birds/management/managed-species/birds-of-conservation-concer; http://www.fws.gov/birds/management/project-assessment-tools-and-guidance/conser; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wm/DSCA/PublicNotices.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=3. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p53→p54: list item ends “… but are not limited to: modification or”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 10 form(s) with 25 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=10, total_fields=25. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 50 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (50 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ic of three wavy blue and green shapes between the"; p1: "... locations: one in Charlotte, NC at 2923 South Try"; p2: "...hic of three stacked, wavy black shapes separating"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=50 (truncated=50, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "if you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 17 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=17. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
